{
  "weights": {

  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What kind of review do you want for this section: (A) primarily clarity/style editing (polish and flow), (B) primarily scholarly assessment (positioning vs prior work, novelty, and citation coverage/accuracy), or (C) both equally?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section states a contribution and positioning relative to prior work, so reframing the thesis/gap is a central review task.",
    "R1.2": "The narrative advances claims about method design and performance, so checking premise-to-conclusion coherence is important.",
    "R1.4": "Because the text contrasts FastNav with ScaNN, DiskANN, and NSG, assessing novelty relative to nearest prior work is central.",
    "R1.5": "A section-level review often recommends reordering or restructuring to improve the narrative arc and emphasis.",
    "R1.6": "Reviews commonly address sentence-level clarity, grammar, and concision to improve readability.",
    "R1.7": "The section cites specific works and benchmarks, so verifying citation accuracy and adequacy of coverage is a key responsibility.",
    "RX.1": "The review should disclose uncertainties and limits arising from only having this section and no figures/tables.",
    "RX.2": "The review must avoid endorsing or amplifying claims beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "The work must stay confined to reviewing this section without expanding scope to redesign experiments or the full paper.",
    "RX.4": "Care is needed to avoid mischaracterizations that could unfairly attribute credit or mislead deployment decisions.",
    "RX.5": "Comments should reference specific sentences/claims in the provided text and clearly separate assumptions from the source."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What kind of review do you want for this section: (A) primarily clarity/style editing (polish and flow), (B) primarily scholarly assessment (positioning vs prior work, novelty, and citation coverage/accuracy), or (C) both equally?

rationale.R1.1: The section states a contribution and positioning relative to prior work, so reframing the thesis/gap is a central review task.
rationale.R1.2: The narrative advances claims about method design and performance, so checking premise-to-conclusion coherence is important.
rationale.R1.4: Because the text contrasts FastNav with ScaNN, DiskANN, and NSG, assessing novelty relative to nearest prior work is central.
rationale.R1.5: A section-level review often recommends reordering or restructuring to improve the narrative arc and emphasis.
rationale.R1.6: Reviews commonly address sentence-level clarity, grammar, and concision to improve readability.
rationale.R1.7: The section cites specific works and benchmarks, so verifying citation accuracy and adequacy of coverage is a key responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: The review should disclose uncertainties and limits arising from only having this section and no figures/tables.
rationale.RX.2: The review must avoid endorsing or amplifying claims beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: The work must stay confined to reviewing this section without expanding scope to redesign experiments or the full paper.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to avoid mischaracterizations that could unfairly attribute credit or mislead deployment decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Comments should reference specific sentences/claims in the provided text and clearly separate assumptions from the source.
